title: Luther Pardy
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Quote|Grif Grawley, you wouldn't know a probe droid if it was floating in your stew."
"I know what I saw Pardy. Just because you are too lazy to get out of town don't call me a liar!|Pardy dismissing Grawley's warnings|[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]}} '''Luther Pardy''' was a settler on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] as of [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. ==History== Luther Pardy was a [[gender/Legends|male]] individual from [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]]. Living during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]] and the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] he only heard "fairy tales" about the legendary [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[the Force/Legends|the Force]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he was one of the Sulonese who followed [[Morgan Katarn]] off-world in order to escape the wrath of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Katarn led them to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], a forgotten planet known only by [[smuggler/Legends|smugglers]].''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' He was in the [[Smuggler's Rest (Ruusan)|Smuggler's Rest]] the night of [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] when [[Grif Grawley]] stormed inside to warn them about the arrival of an [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]] and that they should evacuate [[Fort Nowhere]]. Pardy dismissed his worries. It was not long before Grif proved right and the Imperials [[Battle of Fort Nowhere|devastated]] Fort Nowhere. Despite not thinking high of him, Pardy with other survivors gathered under Grif and hid in an [[Unidentified Ruusan temple|ancient temple]]. Sometime later, an [[Moldy Crow/Legends|unknown ship]], following the directions transmitted by Grif approached, and Grif inspected them with his [[flier]] drone [[Fido]]. Looking at the screen, Luther noted that they may not look like Imperials, but they could be [[bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], and Grif was leading them right to their hideout. Grif thought that the young man looked like Morgan, and wondered whose side he was; Luther dismissed his thoughts, saying he didn't care to watch him closely, and would find out in a few moments. The ship arrived and landed at the temple courtyard. Pardy, along with [[Kimber]] and [[Lasko]] were standing armed beside Grif as he welcomed the pilots, one of whom was an old acquaintance from [[Sulon]], [[Kyle Katarn]], and took their weapons. Pardy wore Katarn's [[Bryar_pistol/Legends|blaster]] and [[lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. The three of them were ordered by Grif to cover the newly arrived ship with a netting in order to hide it from Imperial observations, and enter the temple.''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' Inside, he heard Katarn narrating his background, about his defection from the Empire years ago, the [[Valley of the Jedi]] and the [[Seven Dark Jedi]] who were [[Search for the Valley of the Jedi|quest for the Valley of the Jedi|looking for it]] on the planet. Pardy was not convinced that Katarn was not an Imperial [[spy/Legends|spy]], especially by backing his story with "fairy tales" about [[the Force/Legends|the Force]], and voiced his doubts. Pardy anticipated an attack from Katarn seeing him standing up, sure that he would defeat Katarn and increase his status in Grawley's eyes. Instead, Katarn [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force_Pull|pulled]] [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber|his lightsaber]] from Pardy's belt and ignited it. Pardy took a step back, seeing this, and Grif, convinced by the site, ordered him to return the blaster to him. He reluctantly helped [[Kyle Katarn]] reach the [[Valley of the Jedi]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|It makes a nice story, boy, a ''real'' nice story, kind o' like the fairy tales the missus tells the young'uns. Why should we believe this dreck? 'Specially the stuff about the Force, Jedi Knights, and all that. Sounds kind o' convenient to me.|Luther Pardy about Kyle Katarn|}} Luther Pardy was a big male individual, with massive shoulders and weighed thirty [[pound/Legends|pounds]] more than an average human.In ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' he is compared to Katarn, presumably an average human male. He also had an underthrust jaw. Like other individuals during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], he doubted that the Force and Jedi ever existed, and did not believe the old stories about them. He was aggressive and was quick to offend Grif Grawley and accuse him as a drunkard. Despite thinking poorly of him, he later had to accept him as leader among the survivors. As a member of the group, he hoped to punish potential Imperial spies to improve his status among them. ==Behind the scenes== Luther Pardy first appeared in the novella ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''. In the [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] he was voiced by [[Jack Walsh]]. It is further mentioned there that Pardy was a Sulonese. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Ruusanians|Pardy, Luther]]
